skill.name: git-workflow
skill.description: Guides AI assistants through Git branch naming, pull request structure, PR quality checks, and merge strategy selection.
skill.summary: Create clearer Git branches, pull requests, and review workflows.
skill.author: Joseph OBrien

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported shell execution, weak cryptography, reconnaissance, and hardcoded URL patterns. Review found these are Markdown examples, placeholders, checklist text, or Claude Code attribution links, with no executable script, no active network request, and no prompt injection evidence.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backticks Flagged as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The reported external command locations are Markdown code fences, inline code examples, or branch and PR templates. No executable Ruby, shell script, or command invocation is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are documentation syntax and examples only. There is no runnable file, command dispatcher, shell interpolation, or user-input execution path.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Claude Code Links Are Static Documentation
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URLs point to Claude Code attribution text inside pull request templates. They are not fetched, posted to, or used for data transfer by the skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs appear only as Markdown link targets in generated PR text. No code performs network I/O or sends repository data to these URLs.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Security Terms Misread as Dangerous Behavior
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography and reconnaissance alerts correspond to prose, placeholders, or example PR content. No evidence found of cryptographic API use, network scanning, or malicious intent.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found only Markdown content at these locations. The files contain no implementation that could run crypto operations or perform network reconnaissance.

content.value_statement: Teams lose review time when branches, PR descriptions, and merge choices are inconsistent. This skill gives structured Git workflow guidance for clearer pull requests and safer review habits.
actual_capabilities: Drafts structured pull request titles and bodies using conventional formats.
Recommends branch naming patterns for features, fixes, refactors, docs, tests, and chores.
Builds PR checklists that cover tests, documentation, screenshots, and breaking changes.
Compares squash, merge, and rebase strategies for common workflow situations.
Reviews pull request quality against summary, test plan, issue link, and migration criteria.
limitations: Does not execute Git commands or inspect repositories by itself.
Does not replace project-specific contribution rules or protected branch settings.
Requires the user to provide change context, issue numbers, and test results.
Cannot verify CI status, review approvals, or merge permissions without external tools.

use_cases.title: Prepare Feature Pull Requests
use_cases.description: Turn feature notes into a clear PR title, summary, test plan, and checklist before review.

use_cases.title: Standardize Team Git Conventions
use_cases.description: Define branch naming, PR sections, and merge strategy guidance for a shared workflow.

use_cases.title: Improve Review Readiness
use_cases.description: Check whether a pull request explains the change, documents risk, and lists useful validation steps.

prompt_templates.title: Draft a Basic PR
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner prompt for a small change.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a pull request title and body for this change: [describe the change]. Include summary, motivation, test plan, and checklist.

prompt_templates.title: Name a Branch
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when starting work from an issue.
prompt_templates.prompt: Suggest three branch names for issue [number] about [short description]. Use the skill branch naming format and explain the best choice.

prompt_templates.title: Review PR Quality
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this pull request draft for clarity and completeness: [paste draft]. Identify missing context, weak test steps, and unclear risks.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Team Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Create Git workflow guidance for a team that uses [release model]. Include branch naming, PR templates, review rules, and merge strategy recommendations.

output_examples.input: A developer asks for a PR draft for an OAuth login feature.
output_examples.output: A conventional feature title, a short summary, motivation tied to the user login flow, a test plan, and a review checklist.

output_examples.input: A team lead asks for branch naming rules.
output: Recommended branch patterns by change type.
Examples for feature, fix, docs, test, and refactor work.
Guidance for using issue numbers and short descriptions.

output_examples.input: A reviewer asks whether a PR is ready.
output_examples.output: A concise readiness review that flags missing tests, unclear migration steps, absent screenshots, and weak issue linkage.

best_practices: Provide the issue number, change type, user impact, and test evidence when asking for PR help.
Adapt generated templates to your repository contribution guide before publishing a pull request.
Use the review checklist before requesting human review or merging.
anti_patterns: Do not use generated PR text without confirming that it matches the actual code changes.
Do not hide breaking changes or migration steps in a generic summary.
Do not choose a merge strategy without checking repository policy and release needs.

faq.question: Does this skill run Git commands?
faq.answer: No. It provides workflow guidance and templates. The user or host tool must run any Git commands.

faq.question: Can it create a pull request for me?
faq.answer: It can draft the PR title and body. It does not submit the PR unless another tool provides that action.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Can it enforce branch protections?
faq.answer: No. It can recommend policies, but repository administrators must configure protections in the hosting platform.

faq.question: Is it only for feature work?
faq.answer: No. It includes examples for features, bug fixes, refactors, documentation, tests, and chores.

faq.question: Does it include security review guidance?
faq.answer: It covers general PR quality checks. Dedicated security review still needs project-specific expertise and tools.
